title: Bounded Promise Concurrency, Queueing, Ordering, Backpressure, and Cancellation · Transfer Build
summary: An independent museum program receives a different audience, command script, unknown JSON payload, cache age, async schedule, accessibility need, Node platform, and injected failure and transfers bounded promise concurrency, queueing, ordering, backpressure, and cancellation into a changed bounded abortable detail-prefetch scheduler. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded abortable detail-prefetch scheduler with automated and judgment evidence. Learners prog…
objectives: Justify prefetch from learner latency and API fair-use evidence and identify views where it must remain disabled.
Set active, queued, byte, request, time, and retry budgets independent of result count.
Track queued, active, settled, canceled, and published tasks with exactly one owner for scheduling and cleanup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 2583 during a clean emitted-package smoke project. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce async queue and concurrency budgets, ordering, partial results, abort, and rejection ownership; each Promise, AbortSignal listener, timer, stream, and handle needs explicit ownership; then inject one redirect, prototype-like key, terminal control, path, file, or signal failure and reject it safely. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Justify prefetch from learner latency and API fair-use evidence and identify views where it must remain disabled.

hints: Name the Pokedex Ts Scheduler Tests precondition or invariant that the lab case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: A lab TypeScript team handles case 2999 during a shared classroom network exercise. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce async queue and concurrency budgets, ordering, partial results, abort, and rejection ownership; no user-visible meaning may rely on color, cursor motion, or pointer input; then change one catch, owned record, export, location relation, or history transition without mutating prior state. Browser code uses pure bounded runtime models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, Node readline, host streams, signals, files, package installs, handle and load claims, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use deferred Promises to force saturation, reordered settlement, abort, duplicate, partial failure, queue drain, and zero-unhandled-rejection evidence.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Fast resolved promises exercise concurrency and backpressure adequately.
